seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Google ADK fails when multi-agent systems lose oversight of decision chains, tool usage, and state integrity across Gemini-powered agents. Circular delegation patterns and session corruption create untraceable decision paths that violate governance policies. Tool mismatches and model switching exacerbate inconsistent outputs, making compliance monitoring impossible without systematic intervention.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent A delegates to Agent B, which delegates back to Agent A without termination, detected in ADK's execution traces
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops burn API quotas and create governance black holes where no agent takes responsibility

howItManifests.scenario: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
howItManifests.symptom: Agents switch between gemini-1.5-pro and gemini-1.5-flash mid-conversation, violating content safety thresholds
howItManifests.impact: Inconsistent safety filtering allows harmful content through weaker model variants

howItManifests.scenario: Session state corruption in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: ADK session_state dict accumulates stale agent contexts across turns
howItManifests.impact: Agents make decisions based on corrupted history, violating data governance and producing hallucinated responses

howItManifests.scenario: Tool response format mismatches across agents
howItManifests.symptom: One agent returns JSON tool response while another expects Pydantic models
howItManifests.impact: Silent failures cascade through agent orchestration, creating untraceable compliance violations

detection.name: Circular Delegation Detector
detection.description: Scans ADK execution traces for repeated agent handoffs exceeding depth threshold

detection.name: Model Switch Safety Drift
detection.description: Monitors Gemini model variants in session to detect safety policy drift

detection.name: Session State Bloat Check
detection.description: Detects session_state exceeding governance size limits

fixes.name: Delegation Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Implement max delegation depth in ADK agent orchestration

fixes.name: Consistent Model Policy
fixes.description: Lock Gemini model variant per session for safety consistency

fixes.name: State Pruning Middleware
fixes.description: Automatically prune session_state to governance limits

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK, automatically scanning every agent execution for governance violations before processing. It maintains cross-session state to detect patterns like circular delegation and model drift that span multiple turns. Vex provides real-time remediation by injecting circuit breakers and state pruning directly into the agent orchestration flow.

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) hooks into ADK's execution pipeline, providing observability into delegation graphs, tool call formats, and session evolution. It generates compliance reports and automatically applies fixes like delegation limits and model locking without code changes. Vex's agent-specific policies ensure each agent's tool usage and decision paths remain within governance boundaries.

For production deployments, Vex provides audit trails linking every ADK trace to specific governance policies violated and automatically remediates by rerouting problematic delegations or injecting safety harnesses.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from google_adk import Agent, before_agent_callback

# Install: pip install google-adk vex-sdk
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex.guard("responsible-ai-governance")
@before_agent_callback
def governance_callback(ctx, agent: Agent, input):
    # Vex auto-detects all 4 failure modes
    violations = vex.scan_adk_context(ctx)
    
    if violations.circular_delegation:
        ctx['max_delegations'] = 3
        ctx['circuit_breaker'] = True
    
    if violations.model_drift:
        ctx['force_model'] = 'gemini-1.5-pro'
    
    if violations.state_bloat:
        ctx['session_state'] = vex.prune_state(ctx['session_state'])
    
    # Auto-fix tool format mismatches
    vex.normalize_tool_schemas(ctx)
    
    return ctx

# Apply to your ADK app
app = ADKApp(agents=[agent1, agent2], callbacks=[governance_callback])

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK and add one callback to govern all your Google ADK agents instantly
